scoring_methodology.methodology: Multi-method computational scoring with Cross-Encoder STS primary

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer cross-attention; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score mapped to 0-10 NIST strength scale.

secondary_methods.name: BERTScore
secondary_methods.role: Token-level audit trail (Precision, Recall, F1). P/R directionality validates relationship type assignments.

secondary_methods.name: Jaccard Token
secondary_methods.role: Lexical baseline (word token intersection/union). Hand-traceable without ML dependencies.

scoring_methodology.reproducibility: Pipeline at strm/onet/strm_scoring_pipeline.py produces identical results for identical inputs. All models are deterministic for pinned versions. All 126 per-FDE rationale files contain complete five-method audit trails.

completeness_check.result: PASS — All 126 in-scope FDEs evaluated. 126 per-FDE rationale files generated with complete multi-method audit trails.

rationale_consistency_check.rationale_type_justification: Semantic used as default per use case (vocabulary divergence between general-purpose O*NET and domain-specific WIDAI). Functional used for 41 FDEs where the outcome/activity matters more than vocabulary similarity — particularly for Abilities (cognitive traits) and Work Activities (behavioral descriptions). No syntactic matches expected given vocabulary divergence.
rationale_consistency_check.result: PASS — Rationale types consistently applied per use case justification.

relationship_distribution.analysis: Distribution is consistent with expectations for a general-purpose framework (O*NET) mapped against a domain-specific framework (WIDAI). High 'Intersects with' rate (65%) reflects O*NET's broader scope intersecting with WIDAI's deeper domain coverage. 'Superset of' (14%) correctly identifies where O*NET defines broader competencies that encompass specific WIDAI KSAs. 'Equal' (3%) found only for work activities with direct WIDAI equivalents. Zero 'Subset of' is correct — O*NET elements are always equal to or broader than individual WIDAI KSAs due to O*NET's occupation-general scope.

strength_distribution.analysis: Computed mean strength of 4.18 for scored pairs reflects the vocabulary and scope gap between a general-purpose taxonomy and a domain-specific framework. The cross-encoder STS model processes both descriptions jointly through cross-attention, capturing semantic relationships that lexical and independent-encoding methods miss. Scores cluster in the 3-5 band (NIST 'roughly as similar as dissimilar'), consistent with the structural relationship between a general occupational taxonomy and a specialized professional framework. The secondary methods (BERTScore, NLI, bi-encoder, Jaccard) are documented in each rationale file for audit and future recalibration.
strength_distribution.result: PASS — Strength scores are computationally derived with documented methodology and full five-method audit trails.

gap_issue_quality.analysis: Six thematic gap clusters identified from 8 individual FDE gap signals. All gap issues include required fields per ADR-014 Step 4. Gaps are appropriately categorized: foundational skills gaps (communication, project management, continuous learning) vs. dispositional gaps (service orientation, conflict resolution) vs. foundational knowledge gaps (ethical reasoning). Synthesis recommendations correctly defer resolution and identify cross-framework validation criteria.
gap_issue_quality.result: PASS — Gap issues are complete and appropriately scoped.

public_facing: Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

no_relationship_audit.analysis: All 23 'No relationship' findings are correctly identified as intentionally out of WIDAI scope. No false negatives detected — every No relationship element is either a physical-world competency, a domain unrelated to data/AI work, a basic cognitive trait not differentiating for professionals, or a general office/commercial skill. None represent gaps in WIDAI coverage.
no_relationship_audit.result: PASS — All No relationship findings are justified.

per_fde_rationale_audit.naming_convention: FDE ID with dots replaced by dashes (e.g., 2-C-1-f.json)

strength_justification_contents.primary_method: Cross-Encoder STS (raw score, mapped strength, final integer)

per_fde_rationale_audit.result: PASS — All 126 rationale files conform to schema with complete NIST fields and WIDAI multi-method scoring extensions.

overall_assessment.summary: STRM-001-ONET meets all QA/QC criteria. 126 FDEs exhaustively evaluated with per-FDE rationale files containing five-method scoring audit trails. Strength scores are derived from Cross-Encoder STS (industry-standard pairwise semantic similarity), with BERTScore, NLI, bi-encoder cosine, and Jaccard token overlap as documented secondary methods. Six gap signals identified with complete issue context and synthesis recommendations. The O*NET STRM successfully validates WIDAI's core domain coverage while identifying targeted enrichment opportunities in foundational professional skills.
key_findings: WIDAI has strong coverage of O*NET's data-relevant competencies — 103 of 126 FDEs have documented relationships.
The 'Intersects with' dominance (65%) correctly reflects the general-vs-specific framework relationship.
Multi-method scoring pipeline (STS primary + 4 secondary methods) produces computationally reproducible strength scores with complete audit trails.
Gap signals cluster around foundational professional skills rather than domain-specific technical gaps — confirming the v0.5.2 domain pool is technically comprehensive.
No WIDAI KSA domains were found to be systematically underrepresented in the O*NET mapping.
All scoring data is preserved in per-FDE rationale files for future recalibration or methodology refinement.
